venues.name: The Restaurant
venues.description: Main dining venue on Deck 4, with 4,144 sq ft and capacity for 192 guests. Modern elegant setting. Open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner with rotating international and regional menus. Complimentary.

venues.name: Il Terrazzino
venues.description: Intimate Italian restaurant on Deck 4 seating just 36 guests — name means 'little terrace,' a homage to Silversea's La Terrazza. Authentic Italian pasta, Old World recipes, and à la carte menu that changes every other day. Open for dinner only; reservations strongly recommended. Complimentary.

venues.name: The Grill
venues.description: Casual restaurant across Decks 4–5 with stunning 270-degree panoramic aft views. Double-height space flooded with light. À la carte international dishes. Open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner; dinner service shifts to a more intimate evening format. Complimentary.

venues.name: Arts Café
venues.description: The first Arts Café deployed in polar expedition cruising — a warm haven on Deck 5 with floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking icy polar landscapes. Open 6 am–6 pm. Specialty coffees, teas, fresh pastries, gourmet bites, sandwiches, salads, cakes. Social hub of the ship after Zodiac excursions. Complimentary.

venues.name: La Dame
venues.description: Silversea's French fine-dining restaurant in partnership with Relais & Châteaux. Features an open kitchen — the first La Dame in the fleet with this design. Intimate setting for only 20 guests. À la carte menu or six-course tasting menu with wine pairing. Dinner only; supplement $60 per person.

venues.name: In-Suite Dining
venues.description: Complimentary 24-hour butler-served in-suite dining. Menu options range from casual fare to opulent options including sustainable caviar and Champagne. Full service delivered to every suite.
